title: Warm Flaky Jamaican Feta Cheese Bake
description: A comforting Jamaican dish featuring cubed feta cheese, perfect for a satisfying meal.

ingredients.name: feta cheese
ingredients.notes: use a block of feta cheese with a high concentration of brine

ingredients.name: cold unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: keep the butter cold to achieve flaky layers

ingredients.name: kosher salt
ingredients.notes: use a flaky, kosher salt for best flavor

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: use freshly ground black pepper for best flavor

ingredients.name: ayurvedic herbs
ingredients.notes: optional, use a blend of ayurvedic herbs for added depth

ingredients.name: rajasic herbs
ingredients.notes: omit, for a more sattvic dish

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, cold butter, and a pinch of salt, using a pastry blender or your fingers to work the butter into the flour until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Add the crumbled feta cheese, thyme, oregano, and ayurvedic herbs (if using) to the bowl, stirring until the cheese is evenly distributed.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and gently knead a few times until it comes together in a cohesive mass.
Form the dough into a rectangle, about 1/2-inch thick.
Roll the rectangle into a tight cylinder, then cut it into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on the desired size of your buns.
Place the buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each bun.
Brush the tops of the buns with a little bit of cold water, then sprinkle with a pinch of salt.
Bake the buns in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until they are golden brown and flaky.
Remove the buns from the oven and let them cool on a wire rack for a few minutes before serving.
